title: Tiny Bird's Epic Journey Across the Pacific
story: A tiny bar-tailed godwit chick made history with an incredible flight. The five-month-old bird soared from Alaska to Australia without stopping. This amazing journey shows how even small creatures can achieve extraordinary feats in nature.

title: Rocket Snaps Earth's First Space Selfie
story: Zoom! A special camera hitched a ride on the V-2 No. 13 rocket in 1946, blasting off from White Sands, USA. As it soared higher than any camera before, it captured a groundbreaking image. On October 24, this plucky camera snapped the very first photograph of Earth from outer space. Our planet's curve peeked out against the vast darkness, giving humans a whole new perspective on our home in the cosmos.

activity_answer: The first photograph of Earth from outer space

Despite the chaos, Jamaicans showed incredible resilience, working together to rebuild their communities in the storm's aftermath.
activity_question: What did Hurricane Sandy do when it reached Jamaica?
follow_up_question: How do you think communities can prepare for hurricanes?

title: Napoleon's Snowy Retreat from Moscow
story: In 1812, Napoleon's grand plan to conquer Russia hit a frosty snag. On October 24, his army clashed with Russian forces at the Battle of Maloyaroslavets near Moscow. The fierce fight marked a turning point. Faced with dwindling supplies and the threat of a harsh winter, Napoleon's troops were forced to retreat. They trudged through deep snow towards Smolensk, their dreams of victory melting away.
activity_question: What forced Napoleon's army to retreat after the battle?
bonus_fact: The retreat through snow became known as the 'Great Retreat', one of history's most famous military withdrawals.

title: A Riveting Start for Lady Liberty
story: Clang! The Statue of Liberty's journey began with a single rivet. On October 24, 1881, Levi P. Morton, the US ambassador to France, had the honor of hammering in the very first one. This small act marked the start of building the colossal copper statue that would become a symbol of freedom in New York Harbor.
activity_question: Who drove the first rivet into the Statue of Liberty?
bonus_fact: The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France to the United States.

title: Striking Innovation: The First American Match
story: Sparks flew in 1836 when Alonzo Dwight Phillips struck a brilliant idea. This clever inventor from Springfield, Massachusetts, created the first phosphorus friction match in America. On October 24, Phillips received a patent for his fiery invention, lighting the way for easier and safer fire-starting methods across the country.
activity_question: What did Alonzo Dwight Phillips invent?
follow_up_question: How do you think people started fires before matches were invented?
